Hi, I'm Kevin Halbert, and I hope you got a lot of value out of the previous exercise. That exercise is meant to a lot to a lot of people, so I really hope you did fill out the, wrote right out the letter, stuffed and stamped the envelope and sent it to yourself. There's a lot of people that's had a very profound effect, and he didn't just do that for entertainment value or to take up and waste people's times. It drove home the depth of the value of the power of words. In fact, I keep this on my wall. It's a letter that Parasol and Propolis wrote and sent us that he wrote when, just after our father passed away, and this is what he had to say from Parasol and Propolis. We all know that Gary was an amazing teacher, and I think what set him apart from others is that he didn't just teach things so that you'd understand them on an intellectual level. No, Gary would actually make you experience the lesson so that you'd feel it deep in the core of your being. I remember at a copywriting seminar in 1996, he was trying to impress upon us how two different sales letters could have a drastic effect, drastic difference in response. Now, Gary could have gone, done what most seminar guys do and simply shown us two sales letters and told us that letter number one outpolled letter number two by 75%. But instead, Gary did something much more powerful. He gave us pens and paper and made us write letters to our mothers. The first letter told mom about how special she was and said, I remember you in that flower dress you wore on my 10th birthday. As Gary dictated the words and I wrote them down, I pictured my mother getting a big smile on her face when she received my letter. When we were done, Gary had his folder letter stuffed in an envelope and dropped it in the mail. Then he dictated a second letter which went something like this. Dear mom, I despise you. You are nothing more than a wrinkled up old bitch as far as, and as far as I'm concerned, the biggest waste of skin God ever created. As you might imagine, the people who did this little exercise really learned the lessons. I spoke to several people who tell me they saved those letters and still have them 11 years later. I still have mine, which brings up the final thing I want to say today. And that's the observation that all roads lead to Gary Halbert. Ask anyone who's successful in this business to name the people who influenced them. And I bet every single person names Gary as an influence. For me, that influence was profound. In fact, Gary's the whole reason I became a copywriter. Had I not stumbled upon his writings one day in 1990, I may have never gotten into this business. And who knows where I'd be today. Maybe I'd still be floundering or long, dead broke and trying to figure out what to do with my life. Gary was truly a giant and we're all going to miss him. So the point I'm reading this is that this had a very powerful effect. If you don't know who Parasilm Propolis is, he's one of the only single dozen copywriters that have been able to successfully write controls for boardroom that persist and hold out their position for years. So this isn't something to take lightly. And I hope you don't find it beneath you to do these exercises, write these letters out, and don't find it below your pay grade because there's very little chance that you're most successful in them and they found this to be a breakthrough for them in the depth of understanding and getting it and getting the keys to what makes things work. So this next writing exercise is coming up. I hope you'll do it. If you can, I'd prefer if you could do me a favor and write it out on a legal pad. You don't have to, but don't let that stop you. But definitely write the letter out and don't put anything else on the letter. Just the words that my dad dictates that you should write out. And I think if you've got the value of the Dear Mom letter, or let's say you didn't do the exercise and kind of wish you did, this is another moment. You only get one first shout at doing this. So I would highly suggest that you write this letter out, have it ready, and there's something I want to share with you because it was something I did during a presentation and it was a little something extra I did with this exercise that my dad is about to share with you. And people came up to me and emailed me and wrote me and told me that it had a strong effect on them too. So if I want you to be a part of that experience and I hope you get the value out of it. And so let's go into this video and then I'll come back in and you'll see me and we'll go through this little piece of extra that I want to add on to this exercise. So enjoy.
